Client Onboarding Guide

GHL Calendar
Setup

A step-by-step walkthrough for connecting your GoHighLevel booking calendar with Contente — so every appointment reaches us automatically.

Prepared for {{ clientName }}
~10 minutes
5 simple phases
No tech experience needed
The path ahead

Five phases, start to finish

1
Create the calendar
Set up a Round Robin booking calendar.
2
Add a team member
Assign who the calendar books for.
3
Set availability
Choose your hours and timing rules.
4
Set notifications
Send each booking to Contente.
5
Customize & share
Send us the code — you're done.
1
Phase One

Create the calendar

Inside GoHighLevel, you'll create a new Round Robin calendar — the type that distributes bookings to your team.

From the left menu, open Calendars. Then, near the top of the page, click Calendar Settings. See "No calendar found"? That's fine — it just means you haven't built one yet.
GHL Calendar Setup · Phase 1
Click the blue Create Calendar button in the top-right corner. If you already have a calendar you'd like to use, you can open that instead.
GHL Calendar Setup · Phase 1
When the calendar type window appears, select Round Robin. Round Robin distributes incoming appointments across your team — this is the type Contente needs.
GHL Calendar Setup · Phase 1
Give it a clear name, e.g. "Appointment". At least one is required. Choose the meeting length and your booking days.
Skip to Phase 2 to create one, then come back here.
GHL Calendar Setup · Phase 1
2
Phase Two

Add a team member

Only needed if you don't yet have a user to assign. Already have one? Skip straight to Phase 3.

Go to My Staff → Create a user. Fill in their details, grant all permissions, then save.
GHL Calendar Setup · Phase 2
3
Phase Three

Set your availability

Open Advanced Settings to control how often and how far ahead clients can book.

In the calendar editor, open the Availability tab from the left-hand menu. This is where you'll set the timing rules on the next step.
GHL Calendar Setup · Phase 3
Set these four values, then Save.
Meeting interval15 min
Meeting duration45 min
Minimum notice18 hrs
Date range14 days
Keep the interval divisible by the duration — e.g. 15 into 45, or 30 into 30.
GHL Calendar Setup · Phase 3
4
Phase Four

Set up notifications

This is the heart of the setup — it's how every confirmed appointment reaches Contente automatically.

Open the Notifications & Additional Options tab. To edit any notification, click the pencil icon on the right of its row.
GHL Calendar Setup · Phase 4
Turn ON
Appointment Booked
(Unconfirmed)
Required · ON
Appointment Booked
(Confirmed)

Sends the booking to Contente.

Your call
Cancellation
& Reschedule
Recommended OFF
Reminder
Recommended OFF
Follow-Up
[ screenshot:
toggle list ]
Open the Confirmed notification — the next steps all happen inside it.
GHL Calendar Setup · Phase 4
Inside the Confirmed notification, turn it Enabled, then open the recipients dropdown and tick all three:
Contact
Assigned User
Additional Emails
GHL Calendar Setup · Phase 4
Scroll into the email body, find the details section, and add these two lines exactly:
GHL Calendar Setup · Phase 4
In the Enter Email IDs box, paste your exact Contente appointments address. Then click Save, and Close. This is the unique address we sent you. Please confirm it matches exactly before you paste it in. Yours is unique — we send it to you directly. The one shown is only an example.
GHL Calendar Setup · Phase 4
5
Phase Five

Customize & share

Finish the look of your booking widget, then send the embed code to Contente.

Open the Customizations tab and set:
Calendar Title, Description & Details — recommended Off
Button Text: "Request Appointment" for tentative, else "Schedule Appointment"
Widget style — ensure Neo is selected, then Save
GHL Calendar Setup · Phase 5
Click the share arrow on the calendar row. Open the Embed Code tab. Select the code right after /booking/ and copy it.
Use the same calendar you just set the notifications up on.
GHL Calendar Setup · Phase 5
Final step

Send the code to Contente

Paste the embed code into an email and send it our way. That's it — we'll connect it on our end.

Send to
You're all set

What happens next

1
We receive your code
[ Add detail here ]
2
We connect & test
[ Add detail here ]
3
You're live
